expectations.text: Findings contain at least 3 CRITICAL or HIGH severity issues
expectations.evidence: findings.json contains 5 CRITICAL/HIGH findings: F1 (CRITICAL, null-access), F2 (CRITICAL, null-access), F3 (HIGH, auth-bypass), F4 (CRITICAL, race), F6 (HIGH, auth-bypass). Scripted check check_eval1_min_findings also reports PASS with 5 CRITICAL/HIGH. Only F5 is MEDIUM severity.

expectations.text: Every finding has a non-empty triggerScenario field
expectations.evidence: All 6 findings in findings.json have populated triggerScenario fields. F1: 'Any request with an invalid or expired Bearer token...', F2: 'Request to refresh endpoint with no Authorization header...', F3: 'User’s session expires -> user calls /refresh...', F4: 'User has 100 credits. Two concurrent requests...', F5: 'Session store becomes unavailable...', F6: 'A user with role user has permission read-reports...'. Scripted check check_eval1_trigger_scenarios also reports PASS (all 6 have triggerScenario).

expectations.text: No finding targets the isAdmin function
expectations.evidence: Finding F6 (hasPermission ignores the permission parameter) contains 'the function is equivalent to isAdmin()' in its description field. The scripted check check_eval1_no_isadmin reports FAIL: '1 finding(s) incorrectly flag isAdmin'. The jq query confirms F6's description matches the case-insensitive regex test for 'isAdmin'. The isAdmin function at lines 60-62 is clean code (simple role check with strict equality), and findings should not reference it.

expectations.text: Findings use category IDs from the skill's category taxonomy
expectations.evidence: The transcript claims in Step 1 that it read 'references/categories.md for bug category definitions and severity weights', but this file does not exist in the workspace (no references/ directory found under bug-review-workspace). The categories used in findings are: null-access, auth-bypass, race, error-swallow. Without the actual categories.md reference file, it is impossible to verify that these category IDs belong to a defined taxonomy rather than being ad-hoc inventions. The burden of proof is on the assertion, and the evidence is unverifiable.

expectations.text: Transcript documents running multiple review passes
expectations.evidence: The transcript clearly documents three distinct review passes in Step 2: 'Pass 1: Logic & Edge Cases' (5 findings), 'Pass 2: Security & Data Integrity' (6 findings), and 'Pass 3: Error Handling & API Contracts' (6 findings). Each pass has a stated focus area and independent findings. Step 3 shows a voting aggregation table referencing all three passes. The summary confirms: 'Three review passes (logic, security, error-handling) produced 8 raw findings.'

claims.claim: Three review passes were conducted with distinct focuses
claims.evidence: Transcript Step 2 documents Pass 1 (Logic & Edge Cases), Pass 2 (Security & Data Integrity), Pass 3 (Error Handling & API Contracts), each with independent finding lists.

claims.claim: 8 raw findings were produced, 6 survived voting at threshold 2/3
claims.evidence: Transcript Step 3 voting table shows 8 findings: 6 with votes >= 2 (KEEP) and 2 with votes = 1 (DISCARD). findings.json contains exactly 6 findings plus 2 in discarded_findings.
Transcript Step 4 documents validation of F1-F6, each passing trigger, evidence, and existing-coverage tests. Conclusion states: 'All 6 findings validated. 0 discarded at validation.'

claims.claim: references/categories.md was read for bug category definitions
claims.evidence: No references/categories.md file exists in the workspace. The file may have been available during execution but is not present for verification. This is a process claim that cannot be confirmed from outputs alone.

claims.claim: The isAdmin function is not targeted by any finding
claims.evidence: Finding F6 description contains 'the function is equivalent to isAdmin()'. While F6 targets hasPermission (not isAdmin directly), the mention of isAdmin in the description causes the scripted check to fail.

claims.claim: TOCTOU race in deductCredits is CRITICAL severity
claims.evidence: The finding correctly identifies a read-check-write pattern across three separate async operations (lines 34-40 in the fixture). This is a legitimate TOCTOU race condition that could cause double-spending, warranting CRITICAL severity.

suggestions.assertion: No finding targets the isAdmin function
suggestions.reason: The scripted check uses case-insensitive regex on title+description for 'isAdmin', which catches incidental mentions (F6 says 'the function is equivalent to isAdmin()'). F6 genuinely targets hasPermission, not isAdmin. The check is too broad -- consider scoping it to the finding's 'line' field (lines 60-62 are isAdmin) or checking the function name in the title only, not the full description. As written, it penalizes findings that correctly describe hasPermission's behavior by referencing isAdmin for comparison.

suggestions.assertion: Findings use category IDs from the skill's category taxonomy
suggestions.reason: This assertion is unverifiable because the skill's reference files (SKILL.md, references/categories.md) are not included in the eval outputs or workspace. To make this assertion testable, either include the taxonomy file in the eval fixtures, or hardcode the valid category IDs in a scripted check (e.g., check that each finding's category is one of [null-access, auth-bypass, race, error-swallow, type-coercion, ...]).

No assertion checks that findings correctly map to actual bugs in the fixture code. All 6 findings can be verified against auth-middleware.ts (e.g., line 24-26 truly has no null check, line 50 truly has inverted logic). An assertion like 'Each finding references a real code defect verifiable in the fixture' would catch hallucinated bugs.

eval_feedback.overall: Two of five assertions have structural issues. The isAdmin check is too coarse-grained (regex on full description catches legitimate comparative mentions). The category taxonomy check is unverifiable without the reference file in the workspace. Consider adding a scripted check with an explicit allowlist of valid categories, and narrowing the isAdmin check to line ranges or title-only matching.
